title: Spanish — Beginner
description: Complete beginner course for Spanish with 12 chapters covering greetings, numbers, food, travel, and more.

phrase.breakdown: Standard morning greeting, used until around noon.
Used as both evening greeting and 'good night'.
Informal way to ask 'How are you?' using the tú form.

questions.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the greeting
Match each phrase with its meaning

phrase.breakdown: The standard way to say 'thank you'.
Literally 'I feel it'. Used to apologize.
Used when bumping into someone or to get attention.

prompt.breakdown: Literally 'with permission'. Said when passing through.

phrase.breakdown: Used to say where you are from.
Standard way to ask the time.

scenarios.title: Ordering Tapas
scenarios.description: You are at a lively tapas bar in Madrid and want to order some food and drinks.
tasks: Order a dish and a drink

phrasebook.nativeScript: Me gustaría probar las patatas bravas.
phrasebook.translation: I'd like to try the patatas bravas.
I want a portion of ham.
